content: how do we promote diversity and inclusion on the server?

Christian Williams said:

how do we promote diversity and inclusion on the server?

By not making this community relevant only to researchers! If your target group is uniform, so is the community.
yes, this is definitely open to everyone who likes categories.
And so it should be advertised to non-researchers as well. Otherwise the openness might remain ceremonial, right?
everyone here right now came from twitter, I believe many are not researchers.
anywhere you can think to advertise - within reason - please feel free.

Invite people who are interested and lend your time. They probably won't be researchers, but if you have spare cycles, you can help them get up to speed, and then they'll turn around and do the same. You don't really promote as much as you actively include and apply force to the distribution. Just saying "we're diverse and inclusive" is not enough!
Maybe one thing we could do is make a stream explicitly for "dumb" questions, for questions you have but might feel like you ought to already know it or some nonsense like that.
"oh wait, sorry yall, what's a monoidal category?" should be appropriate somewhere.
This is definitely a good idea - the Lean Zulip has a super friendly "new users" stream and I think it's one of the best things about it
What should it be called? Basic Questions?

I think it's possible to subscribe new members to the Basic Questions by default. That's at least how the lean zulip does it, since new members (especially those with no experience with Zulip) are unlikely to find the stream and would post their questions in General else.
